## Architecture Context
This PR formalizes the empirical baseline tracking methodology required by Epic #10537. It implements a deterministic `wc -c` measurement approach to quantify the exact loaded-surface payload of PR review cycles.

## Changes
- Created `measurement-methodology.md` to document the exact measurement scope and execution procedure for capturing cold-cache and warm-cache loaded bytes.
- Created `pr-review-baseline-2026-04.md` to serve as the centralized longitudinal tracker for the required 10-cycle measurement phase.
- Inserted an early-notice alert in `pr-review-guide.md` to ensure all reviewers execute the measurement protocol before beginning their review.

## Gating Status
Contributes to #10540. (Note: AC5b is longitudinal and remains ongoing across the next 10+ review cycles).

> ### Strengths

> ### §7.1 Minimum-One-Challenge
> 
> Three substantive concerns + one suggestion:
> 
> 1. **Tokenizer secondary-metric ambiguity.** §1 says: *"(only if explicitly requested and stated alongside the primary byte count)."* The methodology doesn't name a canonical tokenizer. If different reviewers use different tokenizers (tiktoken vs sentencepiece vs model-specific), secondary metric is incomparable across cycles. Either name a canonical (e.g., tiktoken `cl100k_base`) OR drop the tokenizer mention entirely from the methodology — the optional framing as written invites incomparable data.
> 2. **AC5 framing-vs-deliverable mismatch.** Sub 1 #10540's AC5 reads *"Pre-extraction baseline captured across the next 10 PR review cycles using AC1 methodology."* This PR ships the methodology + tracker file but cannot capture 10 cycles in a single PR — that's longitudinal. The PR's own §3 says the gating mechanism IS the longitudinal capture, which is correct architecturally. But AC5 as currently worded reads as one-shot. Suggest splitting AC5 in the parent ticket: AC5a (methodology + tracker shipped — this PR satisfies) + AC5b (10 cycles of data captured — longitudinal, satisfied across subsequent PR reviews).
> 3. **Audit payload measurement scope variance.** §2.1.3 reads *"any extracted code diffs, conversation histories, and issue bodies ingested to perform the review."* This is correct but variable per review. Methodology should explicitly state that variance is expected AND the per-cycle Components Breakdown column captures it — turning variance from a measurement bug into a data dimension. One-sentence addition to §2.1 would close this.
> 
> ### Required Actions (Blocking)
> 
> - [ ] **(R1) Tokenizer canonical name OR removal.** Either name `tiktoken cl100k_base` (or equivalent) as the canonical tokenizer in §1, OR drop the optional-tokenizer mention entirely. The current framing invites incomparable data. Strong preference for *removal* given the epic Out-of-Scope note already deprioritizes tokenizer measurement.
> - [ ] **(R2) AC5 split-or-rephrase in parent ticket #10540.** Either edit #10540's AC5 to split into 5a (methodology + tracker — one-shot) and 5b (10-cycle data — longitudinal), OR rephrase to *"Methodology + tracker file ship; longitudinal 10-cycle baseline data is captured incrementally across subsequent PR review cycles per the gating mechanism in §3."* Substantive accuracy of AC matters — Sub 2 will check AC5 status.
> 
> ### Suggestions (Non-Blocking)
> 
> - **(S1)** Add to methodology §2.1: *"Audit payload size varies per review; the per-cycle Components Breakdown column captures this dimension. Variance is expected, not a measurement defect."* One sentence; closes the scope ambiguity without scope creep.
> - **(S2)** Methodology drift sync invariant (mirrors Epic #10537 AC10): when methodology in `measurement-methodology.md` changes, the early-notice callout in `pr-review-guide.md` and the tracker file's column schema must update in sync. > ### Depth Floor
> 
> I actively challenged the methodology against five failure modes:
> 
> 1. **Review-cycle write-path inversion:** §4 tells every reviewer to commit tracker rows. That silently converts normal PR review into repository mutation, ticket/branch/commit work, and likely redundant PR churn.
> 2. **Static-vs-dynamic measurement conflation:** the methodology sums skill files, templates, diffs, issue bodies, and conversation histories into one primary number. That captures operational loaded surface, but it can obscure whether the actual `pr-review-guide.md` modularization reduced the static skill surface.
> 3. **Longitudinal AC mismatch:** #10540 now correctly says `AC5b` is longitudinal, but the PR body still says all acceptance criteria are satisfied.
> 4. **Variance escape hatch mismatch:** #10540's avoided traps say the baseline may need extension to n=15 or n=20 if n=10 is high-variance. The methodology says exactly 10 cycles.
> 5. **Scope cleanliness:** the branch currently carries generated `resources/content/*` snapshots unrelated to the methodology source payload.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### Rhetorical-Drift Audit
> 
> **Findings:** Request Changes.
> 
> The PR body overstates completion: `This satisfies all Acceptance Criteria for #10540` is no longer accurate after the live issue body split `AC5a` and `AC5b`. This PR can satisfy the one-shot methodology/tracker ACs, but it cannot satisfy the longitudinal 10-cycle baseline capture AC.
> 
> The implementation also says the pilot is blocked until the baseline is captured, which is correct. The problem is the completion claim, not the gating concept.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### Cross-Skill Integration Audit
> 
> **Findings:** Request Changes.
> 
> The early notice in `pr-review-guide.md` is the right visibility surface for Sub 1, but the execution procedure needs a non-intrusive measurement path. A reviewer should not have to mutate the repository while performing arbitrary PR reviews unless that is explicitly the assigned aggregation work.
> 
> Recommended split:
> 
> - Reviewers capture a `[MEASUREMENT]` block in the PR review comment, A2A handoff, or #10540 issue comment.
> - The #10540 owner or a dedicated aggregation PR appends verified rows to `learn/agentos/measurements/pr-review-baseline-2026-04.md`.
> - The tracker file remains canonical, but ordinary review cycles do not become implicit branch-write tasks.
> 
> ---
>

> Pass. The method is native to Epic #10537 and directly traces to the #10429 discussion plus the two independent epic reviews. The live #10540 body also includes the origin session and retrieval hint.

> 
> Pass. `Resolves #10540` targets a normal sub-issue, not an epic. #10540 has parent #10537, labels `documentation`, `enhancement`, `ai`, `testing`, `architecture`, and no `epic` label.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### Required Actions
> 
> To proceed with merging, please address the following:
> 
> - [ ] **R1 — Remove unrelated sync snapshots from the PR branch, or explicitly justify them as required source payload.** The current head includes `resources/content/.sync-metadata.json`, discussion snapshots, issue snapshots, and PR snapshots including #10539/#10541. For this methodology PR, those look like auto-generated sync artifacts and inflate the review surface. The branch should normally keep only `.agents/skills/pr-review/references/measurement-methodology.md`, the guide notice, and the baseline tracker unless the content snapshots are deliberately part of the deliverable.
> - [ ] **R2 — Correct the PR body completion claim.** Replace `This satisfies all Acceptance Criteria for #10540` with precise language such as: `This satisfies the one-shot methodology and tracker ACs; AC5b remains longitudinal and blocks Sub-issue 2 until the baseline rows are captured.`
> - [ ] **R3 — Replace `Commit the log` as the default reviewer procedure.** Define where reviewers record per-cycle measurement data without making every review a repo-mutating task. A dedicated aggregation path can still commit rows to the tracker.
> - [ ] **R4 — Split static skill surface from dynamic review payload in the methodology and tracker schema.** At minimum, record separate components for static skill/template bytes and dynamic PR payload bytes, with total loaded bytes derived from both. Otherwise a large diff or long conversation can hide whether modularizing `pr-review-guide.md` actually reduced the source skill surface.
> - [ ] **R5 — Change `exactly 10 cycles` to a minimum baseline with variance extension semantics.** #10540 explicitly names the avoided trap that n=10 may be too narrow. The architectural fix is to capture measurement in a non-mutating shape (PR review comment with `[MEASUREMENT]` tag, A2A handoff, or sub-issue comment) and route aggregation through a dedicated owner/PR. Without the split, a large diff can hide whether guide modularization reduced static surface. The metric becomes mathematically reproducible but answers the wrong architectural question.
